article_title: FDA Pursues Most Sweeping Food Chemical Safety Overhaul in Decades as GRAS Reform, Dye Phase-Out, and PFAS Limits Converge

content_preview.title: FDA Pursues Most Sweeping Food Chemical Safety Overhaul in Decades as GRAS Reform, Dye Phase-Out, and PFAS Limits Converge
content_preview.summary: The FDA is simultaneously overhauling its GRAS ingredient notification system, phasing out petroleum-based food dyes by year-end, and confronting PFAS contamination in produce, marking the broadest transformation of US food chemical regulation since the 1958 Food Additives Amendment.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Three regulatory initiatives are converging at the US Food and Drug Administration in 2026, together representing the most comprehensive reassessment of food chemical safety since Congress passed the Food Additives Amendment in 1958. The agency is preparing a proposed rule to [end the decades-old practice of allowing companies to self-certify food ingredients as safe](https://cen.acs.org/food/food-ingredients/fda-gras-proposed-rule-food-ingredient-safety/104/web/2026/03) without FDA...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Ambitious and comprehensive analysis covering three parallel FDA initiatives (GRAS reform, dye phase-out, PFAS). Exceptional scope for an Analysis piece with well-organized sections.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 sources manually verified. FDA press announcement confirmed dye phase-out: Citrus Red No. 2 and Orange B immediate revocation, six remaining dyes by end of 2026, four natural alternatives, Kennedy quote. C&EN/ACS confirmed GRAS reform: Mark Hartman 'No one knows' quote, OMB review, late spring/early summer proposed rule. STAT News confirmed Kennedy 'innocent until proven guilty' framing. ScienceDaily confirmed PFAS aging study: PFNA and PFOSA in 95% of participants, DNA methylation, men 50-64, Frontiers in Aging. NPR confirmed dye reformulation challenges and natural alternatives complexities.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified: GRAS voluntary notification system since 1997, nine synthetic dyes, specific dye names and revocation timeline, Kennedy directive March 2025, Better Food Disclosure Act S. 3122, PFAS pesticide study findings (37% of samples, 17 pesticides, 40 produce types, fludioxonil concentrations). EU PPWR PFAS limits August 12 2026 date is consistent with known regulation timeline.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Balanced between reporting on the regulatory momentum and noting structural limitations (voluntary enforcement, jurisdictional gaps between FDA and EPA).
editor_notes.originality: Exceptional editorial synthesis connecting three regulatory tracks that are typically covered in isolation. The structural analysis of why these initiatives face implementation challenges adds genuine value.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Outstanding regulatory analysis with all claims verified. One of the strongest pieces in this batch. Approved.
